title: atriplex laciniata subsp. diffusa
intro: Atriplex laciniata subsp. diffusa, also known as the spreading or creeping saltbush, is a shrub-like plant that belongs to the Amaranthaceae family. It is a traditional medicinal plant used in various cultures for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Atriplex laciniata subsp. diffusa can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for various herbal preparations to be made for medicinal use.
